title: Bhubaneswar turns into a furnace as temperature soars to 41°C - Odisha TV
full_text:
Residents of Odisha’s capital city, Bhubaneswar, reeled under intense heat and uncomfortable weather conditions on Friday as temperatures continued to soar. According to observations by the India Meteorological Department (IMD), the temperature in Bhubaneswar soared to 41°C by 2:30 PM on Friday.
The situation has been aggravated by high humidity that reached 38%, making the weather feel much hotter than the actual temperature. Due to the combined effect of high heat and moisture in the air, the heat index reached around 50°C, causing severe discomfort for people of the capital city venturing outdoors.
The sizzling sun, coupled with high humidity conditions, has led to oppressive heat across Bhubaneswar, with roads and public places witnessing reduced activity during peak afternoon hours. Authorities have advised residents to stay hydrated, avoid prolonged exposure to direct sunlight, and take necessary precautions to prevent heat-related illnesses.

Meanwhile, the weather department has issued an Orange Warning for parts of Balasore, Keonjhar, Mayurbhanj, Kalahandi, Kandhamal, and Nabarangpur districts for June 5 and June 6, cautioning of thunderstorms accompanied by lightning and gusty winds reaching 50 to 60 kmph.
Similar conditions are expected in Balasore, Keonjhar, Mayurbhanj, Nabarangpur, Rayagada, Koraput, Malkangiri, and Gajapati districts on June 6 and June 7.
